gate.title: Updated Terms and Privacy Policy
gate.body: We've updated our legal documents. Please review and accept them to keep using XGSail.

support.title: Support XGSail
support.intro: XGSail is free and open source. If you find it useful, consider supporting its development.
support.fundPublish: Publishing on the app stores (once funding allows)
support.fundScale: Scaling up servers as usage grows
support.summary: Funds go toward app development, publishing on the app stores (once funding allows), and scaling up servers as usage grows.
support.nativeNote: On iOS/Android, support the project through your app store's donation options instead.

reminder.text: Enjoying XGSail? Consider supporting its development.

landing.heroSubtitle: Record, replay and analyze your sessions and races — wind, polars, maneuvers and leaderboards in one place.
landing.feature1Body: Record a session straight from your phone, even with the screen locked — native iOS/Android app only.
landing.feature2Body: Relive every session on the map with speed, wind and maneuver overlays.
landing.feature3Body: Polars, VMG, tack and gybe comparisons built from your own recorded data.
landing.feature4Body: Manage race days and results with automatic scoring and a live leaderboard.
landing.feature5Body: Share activities with your crew, group or club, with roles and permissions to match.
landing.feature6Body: The device protocol is open and documented — any hardware can integrate. XGSail E1 is supported natively today, with more trackers on the way.
landing.feature7Body: Run your own stack with one command, or use the hosted version at xgsail.com — your data, your choice.
landing.e1Title: An open-hardware tracker built for XGSail
landing.e1Body: An ESP32-based fleet tracker: GNSS, IMU, wind and pressure logging, live fleet position sharing over BLE mesh, and automatic uploads to XGSail. Open hardware and firmware, no vendor lock-in. Currently a build-it-yourself project, not yet a sold product.

e1Highlights.upload: Auto-upload via WiFi or BLE relay

landing.nativeAppsIntro: The same app as an iOS and Android version, with background GPS recording and Bluetooth device pairing.
landing.androidBody: Not on the Play Store yet — download the signed APK straight from GitHub Releases.
landing.iosBody: Not distributable in any form yet, not even for internal testing.
landing.nativeAppsNote: Neither is published on the official stores yet: publishing requires funding (a one-time fee for Google Play, an annual subscription for the Apple Developer Program).
landing.footerAbout: XGSail is an open-source fork of SailFrames, distributed under the Apache License 2.0.

devices.claimHint: Enter this code on the device within the countdown. The code is single-use.
devices.claimExpired: Code expired — start a new claim.
devices.rotateKeyHint: The previous key stops working immediately. The new key is shown only once.
devices.revokeConfirm: Revoke this device? It will no longer be able to upload.

add.nativeOnly: Open the XGSail app to add this device
add.personalOnly: Personal devices only: connect your own watch from your profile

ble.noneFound: No device found. Make sure it's powered on and nearby.

e1.title: XGSail E1
e1.unreachable: Device not reachable over Bluetooth. Make sure it's powered on and nearby.

info.title: About the XGSail E1
info.intro: E1 is an ESP32-based fleet tracker built for XGSail: GNSS, IMU (heel/pitch), wind, and pressure logging on a single board.

bullets.sensors: GPS, IMU, wind and pressure sensors log every session to the onboard SD card.
bullets.display: A sunlight-readable 3.5" color display shows live position, speed and sensor data on the water.
bullets.mesh: An ESP-NOW mesh shares live position with nearby boats for fleet tracking and race-committee on-course-side (OCS) start detection.
bullets.upload: Sessions upload to XGSail automatically over WiFi, or relayed through your phone via Bluetooth when WiFi isn't available.
bullets.power: A hardware power switch and a physical button handle power and recording — no app required on the water.
bullets.openHardware: Firmware and PCB design are open source (Apache 2.0), published alongside XGSail.

info.diyNote: E1 is an open-hardware, build-it-yourself project — not (yet) sold as a finished product. Photos and assembly guides are coming as builds are completed.
info.repoLink: View the E1 hardware & firmware repo

config.title: Configuration
config.pairingHint: To save, hold the device's physical button for 2 seconds, then save within 60 seconds.
config.wifiPassUnchanged: Leave blank to keep it unchanged
config.boatIdLabel: Device name on the onboard network
config.boatIdHint: Just an identifying label (mesh/filename) — it doesn't change which boat sessions are filed under, which is decided when the device was claimed.
config.autoCleanupUploadsHint: When enabled, the device deletes session files from the SD card once they've been successfully uploaded (WiFi or BLE).

error.pairing_window_closed: Pairing window closed: hold the device's physical button for 2 seconds and try again within 60 seconds.
error.bad_json: Communication error with the device. Try again.
error.sd_busy: Device busy (SD card in use). Try again shortly.

calibration.title: IMU calibration
calibration.hint: With the boat level, calibrate to zero heel and pitch.
calibration.confirmLevel: Make sure the boat is still and level before calibrating. Continue?

error.no_imu: IMU sensor not detected on the device.
error.sd_busy: Device busy (SD card in use). Try again shortly.

sessions.noGps: No GPS data for this session

sessions.deleteConfirm: Delete this session and its data?
sessions.polarLegendHint: average speed curve and max-speed-reached curve for the selected wind strength
sessions.maneuverPickStart: Click the track to set the maneuver's start
sessions.maneuverPickEnd: Click the track again to set the maneuver's end
sessions.trimHint: Drag the two handles on the speed chart to pick the start and end
sessions.optimalAnglesNote: From your recorded performance in this session (not the boat's reference polar).

registra.title: Record a GPS track
registra.batteryHint: On some phones (Xiaomi, Huawei, Samsung, Oppo/Vivo/OnePlus...) the manufacturer's own battery saver can still interrupt background recording: if it stops, disable it manually for XGSail in the phone's battery settings.

error.title: GPS unavailable
error.permissionDenied: Location permission denied. Grant XGSail permission to access your location to record a GPS track.
error.locationServicesDisabled: Your phone's GPS is turned off. Enable it in your phone's location settings, then start recording again.

admin.title: Administration
admin.minNativeVersionHint: Blocks the app below this version (e.g. 1.4.0) with a mandatory update screen. Leave empty to disable — set separately for Android and iOS.
